changes.description: AUTHORITY PATH MISMATCH — The 'Authoritative inputs' section lists knowledge_bus/research/medical_reviews/batch2_thyroid_androgen_context_authority_review_v1.md as a file to read. This path does NOT exist in the repository. The actual medical research authority is at docs/Medical Research Documents/Batch_2_Thyroid_Androgen_Context_Authority_Review.md (confirmed present). Cursor must use the existing file at the preferred path. The STOP condition in 'Required medical research authority' correctly points to the preferred path — the STOP will not trigger. However, Cursor must not report a blocking gap for the missing knowledge_bus/research/medical_reviews/ path.
changes.required_action: Cursor: use docs/Medical Research Documents/Batch_2_Thyroid_Androgen_Context_Authority_Review.md as the medical research authority. Report the missing knowledge_bus path as a non-blocking path discrepancy. Do not STOP for this.

changes.description: FT3 LOW enable_lower_bound IS FALSE — signal_library.yaml for pkg_kb47_free_t3_low_low_t3_syndrome has activation_config.enable_lower_bound: false (signal_library.yaml line 51). The signal_evaluator.py at line 181 reads: lower_bound_enabled = bool(activation_cfg.get('enable_lower_bound', False)) — when False, the lower-bound check is skipped entirely. Since FT3 low is a trigger_direction: low signal, it will NEVER activate with enable_lower_bound: false regardless of any other gate. This must be changed to enable_lower_bound: true as part of FT3 low activation in Phase 7. This is explicitly anticipated in the activation readiness register (next_activation_prerequisite: enable_lower_bound review + metadata runtime_context_requirements).
changes.required_action: Cursor: as part of Phase 7 FT3 low activation, set activation_config.enable_lower_bound: true in pkg_kb47_free_t3_low_low_t3_syndrome/signal_library.yaml. Without this change, FT3 low will not activate at all. Verify signal_evaluator.py line 185: 'if lower_bound_enabled and low is not None and primary_value < low: return lower_bound_state' — confirm the flag controls activation.

changes.description: FEMALE-ONLY GATE ENCODING — The runtime_context_evaluator.py has NO 'value_equals' requirement type. To encode 'fail closed if biological sex is male' for FAI high female-only activation, Cursor must use the existing 'disclosure_state' requirement type with allowed_values: [female]. This works mechanically because build_runtime_context_snapshot() stores snapshot['demographic']['sex'] = str(sex).strip().lower() (runtime_context_evaluator.py line 256), and the disclosure_state evaluator checks bucket.get(key) against allowed_values (lines 503-512). Use: {context_type: demographic, key: sex, requirement: disclosure_state, allowed_values: [female]}. This is the only available mechanism without modifying the evaluator. Do not invent a new requirement type unless STOP condition 3 or 8 is triggered.
changes.required_action: Cursor: for FAI high female-only gate, use requirement: disclosure_state with allowed_values: [female] on key: sex in the demographic context bucket. Verify this works by reading runtime_context_evaluator.py lines 503-512 and confirming the sex value stored is lowercase.

changes.description: ADULT-ONLY AGE GATE CANNOT BE ENCODED WITH EXISTING REQUIREMENT TYPES — runtime_context_evaluator.py supports only: present, disclosed, disclosure_state, lab_range_boundary. None of these can enforce a minimum numeric age threshold (e.g. age >= 18). The demographic snapshot stores snapshot['demographic']['age'] as an integer (line 267) but no evaluator requirement type can compare it to a minimum. If Cursor cannot represent 'fail closed if non-adult' deterministically, STOP condition 11 applies for free testosterone low ('free testosterone low cannot be restricted to adult male symptom-gated interpretation'). Cursor must either: (a) add a minimum_value requirement type to the evaluator (allowed as 'strictly justified' backend change), or (b) add a runtime context primitive 'is_adult' (boolean) to the snapshot builder, then gate on that with disclosure_state or present requirement. If neither approach is safe to add in this sprint, free testosterone low must remain inactive and the age-gate gap documented as the blocker.
changes.required_action: Cursor: for free testosterone low adult-only gate, determine whether adding a minimum_value requirement type to runtime_context_evaluator.py or adding an is_adult snapshot primitive is within sprint scope and safe. If it is, implement it. If not, STOP activating free testosterone low and document the adult-gate gap as the blocker in Phase 8.

changes.description: MISSING GATES IN SIGNAL LIBRARIES — All 4 activation target signal libraries have partial runtime_context_requirements that do NOT yet include all gates mandated by Phase 3-6. Cursor must ADD these gates as part of Phase 7 activation. Specific gaps found by reading the signal_library.yaml files: (1) FT3 low — missing thyroid_medication_status gate and energy-availability gates (calorie_restriction_status, fasting_status, under_eating_status, heavy_training_load_status). (2) FAI high — missing pregnancy gate, menstrual/menopause gate, dhea_supplementation exclusion gate, fertility_treatment gate, androgen excess symptom disclosure, female-only sex restriction. (3) Free testosterone high — missing shbg present gate, testosterone present gate, aas_exposure exclusion gate, dhea_supplementation exclusion gate, pregnancy gate, method/assay gate. (4) Free testosterone low — missing testosterone present gate, shbg present gate, male-only sex restriction, adult-only gate, illness/recovery context gate, energy availability context gate, aas_exposure exclusion gate. All these gaps are expected — the packages are governance_promoted_pending_runtime_activation and the sprint is explicitly intended to populate these gates.
changes.required_action: Cursor: treat all Phase 3-6 gate specifications as the authoritative gate set to encode in runtime_context_requirements for each activation target. The signal_library.yaml files have partial gates already — extend them with the missing gates from the phase specifications. Do not partially activate a signal with incomplete gates (prompt non-negotiable constraint).

changes.description: DHEA IDENTITY GATE PRE-RESULT — pkg_kb47_dhea_high_androgen_excess_context/signal_library.yaml line 12 has primary_metric: dhea (KBP-4702). The SSOT at backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml line 1397 confirms dhea and dhea_s are distinct canonical biomarkers. The medical research authority (docs/Medical Research Documents/Batch_2_Thyroid_Androgen_Context_Authority_Review.md line 168-244) gives verdict: VALID_ONLY_AS_WEAK_CONTEXTUAL_SIGNAL and runtime activation recommendation: DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_YET_CONTEXT_MODEL_INSUFFICIENT. The required approval phrase 'APPROVE DHEA-S ACTIVATION IN BATCH2-FULL-COVERAGE-ACTIVATION-1' is ABSENT from the prompt. Therefore the Phase 2 DHEA identity gate result is predetermined: KEEP_INACTIVE_PENDING_DHEA_DHEAS_IDENTITY_REMEDIATION for DHEA high, DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_EVIDENCE_INSUFFICIENT for DHEA low.
changes.required_action: Cursor: confirm the DHEA gate result in Phase 2 as KEEP_INACTIVE for both packages. No DHEA/DHEA-S activation occurs in this sprint. Document that the package primary_metric is dhea (not dhea_s), which is itself the identity ambiguity that must be resolved before any DHEA-lineage activation.

stage_2a_authority_verification.duplicate_authority_sources: NONE — no duplicate authority for any of the 4 activation targets found. Medical research authority exists at one location only (preferred path). Governance files each serve a distinct purpose.

stage_2b_behavioural_hardening.change_type_justified: Activation changes signal_library.yaml activation_config (enable_lower_bound) and runtime_context_requirements. signal_evaluator.py at line 181 reads enable_lower_bound from activation_config — a write to this field in FT3 low's signal_library.yaml changes whether the signal fires. runtime_context_requirements gates written to package signal_library.yaml files are consumed by signal_evaluator.py via passes_runtime_context_requirements() — adding new gates changes which biomarker/context profiles produce signal output. Both changes affect emitted analytical output. Governance register updates and package_manifest.yaml activation_state changes are CONTENT changes wrapped in the same sprint but the signal_library.yaml activation changes are definitively BEHAVIOUR.

stop_conditions_protect_behavioural_corr.stop_3: If required context gates cannot be represented deterministically — STOP. Female-only gate IS representable using disclosure_state mechanism (confirmed). Adult-only gate for FT3 low is NOT required (no age gate on FT3 low). Adult-only gate for free testosterone low NEEDS assessment — if not encodable, STOP activation of free testosterone low only.
stop_conditions_protect_behavioural_corr.stop_11: free testosterone low cannot be restricted to adult male if age gate cannot be encoded — signals partial STOP (free testosterone low stays inactive); does not kill entire sprint

stop_conditions_protect_behavioural_corr.stop_24: Rollback path undefined — rollback is git branch deletion (valid)

determinism_risks_surfaced: RISK 1 (HIGH): enable_lower_bound must be set to true only after all context gates for FT3 low are in place. If enable_lower_bound is set to true without completing the full gate set, FT3 low may emit without required gates. Cursor must complete ALL gate additions before changing enable_lower_bound, or change enable_lower_bound and gates atomically in the same commit.

RISK 2 (MEDIUM): Female-only gate using disclosure_state with allowed_values: [female] correctly suppresses signal for male and unknown-sex patients. But the evaluator checks bucket.get(key) — if sex is stored as 'Female' (capitalised) rather than 'female' (lowercase), the gate fails. runtime_context_evaluator.py line 256 confirms .lower() is applied: snapshot['demographic']['sex'] = str(sex).strip().lower() — this risk is LOW given the lowercase enforcement.

RISK 3 (MEDIUM): Free testosterone low requires both sex=male gate and adult-only gate. If only the sex gate is encoded and the adult gate is deferred/blocked, free testosterone low should remain inactive as a whole — partial gating is forbidden by the non-negotiable constraint 'Do not partially activate a signal with missing gates'.

RISK 4 (LOW): The 5 inactive packages (DHEA high, DHEA low, FAI low, free testosterone pct high, free testosterone pct low) must not have runtime_context_requirements cleared or altered in ways that could accidentally enable them. Their current signal_library.yaml files have partial runtime_context_requirements already — Cursor must only UPDATE their package_manifest.yaml and governance registers to record inactive status; do not touch their signal_library.yaml gate content.

RISK 5 (LOW): Package manifests for activation targets have behavioural_impact: NONE (package_manifest.yaml lines 12-13). This field should be updated to reflect the actual behavioural impact of activation. Failure to update this is a metadata inconsistency but does not affect runtime behaviour.

stop_1_medical_research_missing.rationale: Medical research authority at preferred path docs/Medical Research Documents/Batch_2_Thyroid_Androgen_Context_Authority_Review.md IS present (confirmed). STOP is tied to preferred path only.

stop_2_activation_target_lacks_research_.rationale: All 4 activation targets (FT3 low, FAI high, free testosterone high, free testosterone low) have ACTIVATE_WITH_GATES verdicts in the medical research authority (lines 9-14). The 5 non-activation targets correctly have DO_NOT_ACTIVATE verdicts.

stop_3_context_gates_not_representable.will_trigger: PARTIAL RISK — adult-only gate for free testosterone low may not be representable with existing requirement types. See hardening note 4. All other required gates ARE representable with existing evaluator types.
stop_3_context_gates_not_representable.rationale: Female-only sex gate: representable via disclosure_state with allowed_values: [female] on demographic.sex key. Thyroid medication gate: representable via medication.thyroid_medication_status_disclosed requirement. Energy availability gates: representable via clinical_context.calorie_restriction_status and clinical_context.energy_availability_status primitives (added in BATCH2-FULL-COVERAGE-BUILD-1). Biomarker presence gates: representable via biomarker context_type with requirement: present. Adult-only gate: NO existing requirement type can compare demographic.age (integer) to a minimum threshold — this is the partial risk.

stop_4_answered_no_not_distinguished.rationale: runtime_context_evaluator.py confirms DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_YES, DISCLOSURE_ANSWERED_NO, DISCLOSURE_NOT_ANSWERED are all defined and distinguishable (lines 46-59). disclosure_state requirement type with allowed_values allows specifying which states satisfy a gate.

stop_5_positive_exposure_not_separate.rationale: runtime_context_evaluator.py lines 361-373: aas_exposure_status_disclosed (boolean) and aas_exposure (boolean) are separate keys — disclosure state and positive exposure are separate.

stop_6_ft3_low_cannot_require_tsh_ft4.rationale: FT3 low signal_library.yaml already has tsh and free_t4 as biomarker context_type requirements with requirement: present. These gates exist.

stop_7_ft3_low_cannot_fail_closed_on_ill.rationale: illness_or_recovery_status_disclosed gate exists in FT3 low signal_library.yaml. Energy availability gates (calorie_restriction_status, fasting_status) exist as clinical_context primitives added in BATCH2-FULL-COVERAGE-BUILD-1. These gates can be added to runtime_context_requirements.

stop_8_fai_high_female_only.rationale: Female-only gate is representable using disclosure_state requirement with allowed_values: [female] on demographic.sex. runtime_context_evaluator.py line 256 confirms sex is stored lowercase. This gate will suppress FAI high for male patients.

stop_9_fai_requires_testosterone_shbg.rationale: FAI high signal_library.yaml already has shbg and testosterone as biomarker context_type requirements with requirement: present (lines 62-65).

stop_10_free_testosterone_method_uncerta.will_trigger: RISK — runtime context evaluator has no method/assay-type gate. Cursor must assess whether a method_flag context primitive exists in the snapshot builder, or whether fail-closed default (suppress_signal) is sufficient when method cannot be captured.
stop_10_free_testosterone_method_uncerta.rationale: No method_flag or assay_type runtime context primitive found in runtime_context_evaluator.py. Free testosterone high prompt Phase 5 requires method gate. If method cannot be represented, prompt says 'fail closed if method cannot be represented safely' — suppressing the signal may be acceptable.

stop_11_free_testosterone_low_adult_male.will_trigger: PARTIAL — if adult-only age gate cannot be encoded, free testosterone low must remain inactive. Sprint can still proceed without activating free testosterone low.
stop_11_free_testosterone_low_adult_male.rationale: Male-only gate and symptom gate are representable. Adult-only gate requires age threshold comparison which no existing requirement type supports.

stop_19_diagnosis_wording.rationale: Diagnosis wording is prohibited in the signal_library.yaml explanation and interpretation fields. None of the existing signal_library.yaml files contain diagnosis language. The wording in the libraries uses cautious language: 'contextual low T3 syndrome' not 'hypothyroidism'. Phase 3-6 wording boundaries must be respected in any wording updates.

stop_24_rollback_undefined.rationale: Sprint is on a dedicated branch. Rollback is git branch deletion. New signal_library.yaml gate additions can be reverted. No SSOT changes expected.

hardening_notes: Sprint is correctly classified HIGH / BEHAVIOUR / TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. Medical research authority confirmed at preferred path with all 9 pattern verdicts. All 7 referenced audit papers confirmed present. All 14 governance files confirmed present. All 9 package directories confirmed present and inactive. Signal evaluator enable_lower_bound mechanism confirmed — FT3 low requires enable_lower_bound: true change as part of activation. Female-only gate for FAI high is representable using existing disclosure_state requirement type. Adult-only gate for free testosterone low requires assessment by Cursor — may require a new minimum_value requirement type or is_adult snapshot primitive, or free testosterone low remains inactive. DHEA identity gate result is predetermined: KEEP_INACTIVE (approval phrase absent, research says DO_NOT_ACTIVATE_YET, primary_metric is dhea not dhea_s). Six hardening notes added covering: authority path mismatch (non-blocking), enable_lower_bound mandatory fix, female-only gate encoding mechanism, adult-only gate risk, missing gates in signal libraries, DHEA identity pre-result. Two STOP conditions carry partial risk (stop_10 method gate, stop_11 adult gate) but neither kills the sprint — they gate individual package activations. GPT architectural review and human approval required before merge per HIGH risk route.
